attributes.content:
## Overview

A `SupportTicketForm` card. Renders an isolated view a customer can fill out to file a support ticket. After submission the view confirms what they sent.

## Fields

- `submitterName` â€” the person's name. Required.
- `submitterEmail` â€” their email address. Required.
- `priority` â€” one of `low`, `normal`, `high`, `urgent`. Defaults to `normal`.
- `subject` â€” short summary of the issue. Required.
- `message` â€” the detailed description, long-form text. Required.
- `submitted` â€” boolean; flipped to `true` by the submit action stub.

## Behavior

1. The isolated view shows a form bound to the five user-facing fields above. Each field has an accessible label and the priority field offers the four values as choices.
2. A submit control sits below the form. Clicking it logs the submitted fields (`console.log('submitting ticket:', { ... })`) and sets `submitted = true`.
3. While `submitted` is true, the form is replaced by a confirmation panel: a small tag-style indicator showing the chosen priority, a one-sentence acknowledgement that echoes the subject, and a control to submit another ticket (resets `submitted` to false).

## Design direction

Mid-1990s Geocities aesthetic. Unapologetic. Lean into it:

- Tiled patterned background (inline SVG data URL â€” no external images).
- Clashing neon palette (lime / magenta / cyan / yellow on dark navy or black).
- Comic Sans for body, Impact or similar for headings.
- Beveled borders, drop shadows, sparkle separators between sections.
- A scrolling marquee for the page title.
- A small blink / pulse animation on at least one element.
- A fake visitor counter and a `Best viewed in Netscape Navigator 4.0` style stamp in the footer.
- An `Under Construction đźš§` ribbon somewhere visible.

The palette and typography deliberately do not match the rest of the design system â€” that's the point of this card.

## Out of scope

- Server-side submission. The submit action is a stub.
- Edit-format UI. Only the isolated view is required.
- Real input validation. Whatever the UI primitives provide for free is enough.
- Auto-playing background music. (Very Geocities. Still no.)
- External image or asset URLs. Inline SVG and Unicode characters only.
- **Real QUnit rendering tests.** Write the smallest possible placeholder `.test.gts` â€” a single `module(...)` with one `test('placeholder', (assert) => { assert.ok(true); })`. Do NOT import `setupRenderingTest`, `setupBaseRealm`, `renderCard`, or any other host test helpers; do NOT instantiate the card with `new`. Real rendering tests are a follow-up.

cardInfo.name: Support Ticket Form
cardInfo.summary: A support-ticket submission form rendered in mid-1990s Geocities style â€” tiled backgrounds, neon palette, Comic Sans, scrolling marquee, sparkle separators.
